tenors.narrative: Prediction markets expect WTI to decline 9.4% to a median of $89/bbl by end-April 2026, with substantial uncertainty reflected in a wide interquartile range of $82-$127. The 10th-90th percentile span ($76-$150) suggests markets are pricing in significant tail risks, with roughly equal probability mass on both bullish and bearish scenarios over the 20-month horizon.

By June 2026, markets signal a more pronounced downside bias, with the median falling to $80/bbl (-18.2% from spot) across lower trading volume ($1B). The distribution remains highly dispersed ($61-$197 across the 10th-90th range), indicating elevated uncertainty about longer-term supply-demand dynamics and geopolitical factors.
